questions.question: What is the valid range of values for the CSS opacity property?
questions.explanation: The opacity property accepts values from 0 (fully transparent) to 1 (fully opaque). Values outside this range are clamped.

questions.question: What is a key difference between using opacity: 0.5 and rgba() with alpha 0.5 on a background?
choices: opacity affects the element and all its children; rgba only affects the specific property
rgba affects children but opacity does not
opacity only works on background colors
questions.explanation: The opacity property makes the entire element and all its children semi-transparent, while using rgba() on a background only makes that background semi-transparent without affecting child elements.

questions.question: What side effect does setting opacity to a value less than 1 have on an element?
choices: It removes the element from the document flow
It creates a new stacking context
It forces the element to be inline
questions.explanation: Setting opacity to any value less than 1 creates a new stacking context for the element, which can affect how z-index works for its children.

questions.question: If a parent element has opacity: 0.5 and a child has opacity: 1, what is the child's effective opacity?
choices: 0.5 (the child's opacity is multiplied by the parent's)
questions.explanation: Opacity is multiplicative. A child with opacity: 1 inside a parent with opacity: 0.5 results in an effective opacity of 0.5 * 1 = 0.5. You cannot make a child more opaque than its parent.

questions.question: The CSS ___ property controls the transparency of an entire element and its children.
questions.explanation: The opacity property sets the transparency level for the entire element including all child content, from 0 (fully transparent) to 1 (fully opaque).

questions.question: To make only a background semi-transparent without affecting child elements, use the ___ color function instead of the opacity property.
questions.explanation: Using rgba() on a background-color allows you to set transparency on just the background, leaving child elements at full opacity.
